name: BTC Inventory Reset Reclaim v1
thesis: BTC 4h is the best similar-asset lane for inventory-reset continuation after flush-and-reclaim, rewritten as a broad reclaimable reset instead of an exact oversold event chain. The edge should come from forced position cleanup followed by balance recovery, not from static oversold bounce logic.
invalidation_condition: Abort if reset-and-reclaim setups remain too sparse, if flushes fail to reclaim value quickly enough, or if recovered value does not extend after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price flushes below short-term value, weak inventory is forced out, and continuation becomes tradable only once value is reclaimed and held.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Pure chop often produces inventory flushes that are immediately repaired. A reclaim filter should capture the better resets without requiring crash-only extremes.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 4h is a clean similar-asset lane for reset/reclaim logic because flushes and recoveries tend to be visible without the extra noise of smaller alts.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If flushes become trend continuation rather than inventory resets, the reclaim step will arrive too late or not at all.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If setups engage but extension is small, simplify exits toward faster balance capture before tightening reclaim depth.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; flush and reclaim are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by moderate flush thresholds rather than rare capitulation definitions

long: Final 20% exits on touch of EMA_20 + 0.45 * ATR_14 or 5 bars
short: Final 20% exits on touch of EMA_20 - 0.45 * ATR_14 or 5 bars
